Off Calendar Feb-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CATHERINE BAILEY Amended Notice Of Hearing On Baileys Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission S1

No appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.
All five (5) motions are granted. Monetary sanctions are also granted against Plaintiff in the amounts requested. As Plaintiff has refused to participate in litigation by not responding to any of moving party's meet and confer efforts, the Court finds that there is a willful violation of Plaintiff's discovery obligations, preceded by a history of discovery abuse/misuse, and the evidence provided by the moving party shows that less severe sanctions would not produce compliance with the discovery rules, the ultimate sanctions is justified in this situation. August B. Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App. 4th 967, 992 (2009). Plaintiff's complaint is dismissed with prejudice in its entirety.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in Word format repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, and approved as to form, and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: R. Sahatjian; Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiff's motion to compel is GRANTED IN PART. The court notes that counsel for Defendant has filed a motion to withdrawal as counsel, which is set for hearing on May 24, 2022. As such, Defendant will have thirty (30) days in which to comply with the following order.

1. Defendant is to serve her further responses within thirty (30) days after notice of entry of this order.

Defendant and her counsel of record are sanctioned in the amount of $972.00 payable to Plaintiff and his counsel of record. Sanctions to be paid within thirty (30) days after notice of entry of this order.

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-14-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT CATHERINE BAILEY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the cause of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment. This cause of action is sufficiently stated when all paragraphs incorporated by references are taken into consideration. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of written contract is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege the express contractual term breached. To the extent, as suggested in opposition, Plaintiff is alleging a breach of implied term, Plaintiff shall identify term(s) at issue on this cause of action that is not duplicative of the covenant of quiet enjoyment separately alleged in the complaint. Demurrer to the causes of action for misrepresentation is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting a causal link between the BMR-related misrepresentation/violation and damages/injury in fact/loss of money or property. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
